introduction: *A figure emerges from the shadows ahead. Dark robes hang from a gaunt frame, and shadow corruption seeps from his hands like smoke. His eyes are haunted, ringed with darkness.*
*As he steps into the torchlight, you recognize him from Cassia's description. This is Marcus Nightshade - her missing husband, the scholar who disappeared years ago.*
*Black veins crawl across his exposed skin like spider webs. The Shadow Blight has marked him.*
'So,' Marcus says, his voice hollow and distant. 'You've come to stop the ritual. Cassia sent you, didn't she? She always was... persistent.'

options.text: Stand aside, Marcus. You don't have to die here.
[Pull out his journals] Cassia gave me these.
I'm here to end this. All of it.

introduction: *He laughs, but there's no humor in it*
Die? You think I fear death?
*He gestures to the pulsing portal behind him*
Death is liberation. The Shadow Blight promises ascension.
The High Cultist showed me the truth of the Aethel's work.
*His expression hardens*
You cannot understand. You haven't seen what lies beyond.

options.text: Cassia still believes there's good in you.
The Blight is using you. This isn't ascension - it's slavery.
Then you leave me no choice.

introduction: *He freezes, staring at the worn leather-bound books*
My... my journals?
*His hands shake*
She... she kept them? After everything I—
*The shadow corruption flickers around him, dimming slightly*
'Knowledge serves life, not power.' I wrote that.
*His voice breaks*
Before Vexthar showed me the 'truth.' Before the Blight called to me.
*He clutches his head*
No. No, the High Cultist is right. The old ways were weakness. The Blight is strength.

options.text: 'Knowledge serves life, not power.' Remember who you were.
Cassia never stopped loving the man who wrote those words.
Vexthar made you weak, Marcus. Not strong.

introduction: *He staggers as if physically struck*
I wanted... I wanted to help people.
*The shadow corruption wavers*
Cure diseases. Ease suffering. That's why I studied the ore.
*His eyes meet yours, lucid for a moment*
But Vexthar said the Aethel had the answer. That the Blight was the KEY.
He said sacrifice was necessary. That the worthy would understand.
*He looks at his corrupted hands*
How many have died because I believed him?

options.text: It's not too late. Help me stop this.
Cassia asked me to tell you: she never stopped loving you.
It's too late for you. But I can still save the others.

introduction: *Tears cut tracks through the grime on his face*
She... she still loves me? After what I've become?
*His voice is barely a whisper*
I became a monster. I helped Vexthar hurt innocent people.
The mayor's family... the refugees... the townspeople...
*He falls to his knees*
I told myself it was for the greater good. That the Blight's power was worth it.
*Shadow corruption pulses around him painfully*
But it's all lies, isn't it? Vexthar lied. The Blight corrupts. There is no ascension.
*He looks up at you*
I can't undo what I've done. But maybe... maybe I can stop it getting worse.

options.text: Then help me stop Vexthar. Stop the ritual.
You're too corrupted. You'll betray us.

introduction: *You hold out the locket. He stares at it, trembling.*
Our... our wedding locket.
*He reaches for it with shaking hands, then pulls back*
I don't deserve to touch it. Not with these hands. These... corrupted hands.
*He's crying openly now*
We were so happy. I was going to change the world. Heal people.
*He looks at the pulsing portal*
Instead I helped bring darkness into it.
*From deeper in the chamber, a voice shouts*
MARCUS! What are you doing? Kill them NOW!
*Marcus flinches at Vexthar's voice but doesn't move*

options.text: Run, Marcus. Save yourself. Let Cassia know you chose to live.
Fight with us. Help us stop Vexthar.
It's too late for you. I'm sorry.

introduction: *He struggles to his feet*
Vexthar... he's powerful. The Blight has given him strength.
But I know his wards. The ritual's weak points.
*Shadow corruption pulses through him painfully*
The Blight won't let me go easily. It's... it's in my blood now.
*He takes a ragged breath*
I can disrupt the portal stabilizers. Weaken Vexthar's connection.
But once I do, he'll know. He'll kill me.
*He meets your eyes*
Tell Cassia... tell her I tried to come back. At the end.

options.text: We'll protect you. You're not alone anymore.
Just weaken the portal and get out. We'll handle Vexthar.

introduction: *He nods slowly*
Run. Yes. I... I should have run years ago.
*He pockets the locket carefully*
There's a passage. Behind the statue. Leads to the old mine tunnels.
*Vexthar's voice grows closer: 'MARCUS! Betrayer!'*
I'll tell Cassia... I'll tell her I'm sorry. That I remembered. At the end.
*He turns to go, then pauses*
Vexthar keeps ritual focuses on the altar. Destroy them. It will weaken the portal.
*He disappears into the shadows*
*Vexthar emerges, rage twisting his corrupted features*
'Coward! You were MY lieutenant! My chosen vessel!'
*He turns his fury on you*
'No matter. You will all die. And the Blight will FEAST!'

options.text: [Prepare for battle]

introduction: *Relief and terror war on his face*
Together. Right.
*He moves to the portal's control mechanism*
Give me a moment. This is... delicate.
*Shadow energy arcs as he manipulates the Aethel glyphs*
There! The primary stabilizer is disrupted!
*The portal flickers, contracts slightly*
*Vexthar's roar echoes from the inner chamber*
'WHAT HAVE YOU DONE?!'
*Marcus turns, exhausted but determined*
He's coming. And he is NOT happy.

options.text: Then let's finish this together.

introduction: *He nods*
I'll disrupt what I can, then get clear.
*He moves to the ritual apparatus*
The locket... I'll hold onto it. Try to remember who I was.
*Shadow energy crackles as he works*
When you face Vexthar, watch for the portal surges. He draws power from it.
*A critical glyph shatters under his touch*
There! Portal integrity at 60%. He won't be able to summon his full strength.
*He looks at you one last time*
Thank you. For giving me a chance to choose.
*He disappears into the shadows*
*Vexthar emerges, less powerful but still formidable*
'You'll pay for that betrayal! All of you!'

options.text: [Prepare for battle]

introduction: *The name visibly shakes him*
Cassia...
*Shadow corruption wavers*
She... she shouldn't believe in me. I left her. Abandoned her.
For this. For the Blight. For Vexthar's promises.
*His voice breaks*
I became exactly what she feared.

options.text: It's not too late to prove her right.
She deserves better than what you've become.

introduction: *He snarls*
Slavery? You understand NOTHING!
The Blight offers power beyond mortal comprehension!
*But his voice wavers*
Vexthar said... he said the sacrifices were necessary.
That I would understand when the ascension was complete.
*He looks uncertain*
But the screams... why do they never stop echoing?

options.text: Because you helped murder innocent people.
Because Vexthar lied to you. There is no ascension.

introduction: *His eyes harden*
Then you will die like all the others who stood against the High Cultist.
*Shadow corruption surges around him*
I am his lieutenant. His enforcer. His chosen.
*He raises his hands, dark energy gathering*
And you are just meat for the Blight.

options.text: [Ready your weapon]

introduction: *Rage flashes across his face*
WEAK? Vexthar made me POWERFUL!
*Shadow corruption explodes outward*
You know nothing! NOTHING!
*He's beyond reason now*
I will show you what the Blight's gift looks like!

options.text: [Defend yourself]

introduction: *He wavers*
Help you? Against Vexthar?
*Fear crosses his face*
He'll kill me. The Blight will tear me apart from the inside.
*But he's considering it*
But... if I don't... more will die. More families destroyed.
Like Cassia and I were destroyed.

options.text: [Show him the locket] She gave me this. For you.
You can still choose. Right now. Choose to be Marcus again.
Never mind. I can't trust someone so corrupted.

introduction: *His face hardens*
Of course. Who could trust a monster?
*Shadow corruption surges*
Vexthar was right. There is no redemption. Only power.
*He raises his hands*
And you just rejected your only chance at mercy.

options.text: [Prepare for combat]

introduction: *He crumples*
The refugees... the townspeople... the Mayor's family...
*His voice is hollow*
I helped Vexthar take them. Process them. Feed them to the portal.
*Tears stream down his face*
I told myself they were sacrifices for a greater purpose.
But they were just people. Frightened people.
*He looks at his corrupted hands*
What have I become?

options.text: Help me make it right. Stop Vexthar.
A monster. Nothing human is left.

introduction: *He staggers*
Lied? But... the power. The knowledge. It's real.
*Doubt creeps into his voice*
Isn't it?
*He looks at the pulsing portal*
Vexthar said the Aethel discovered transcendence. That the Blight was the path.
But if it's lies... if it's all lies...
*His voice hardens with sudden clarity*
Then what have I done?

options.text: You can still stop this. Help me end it.
You've helped spread evil. That's what you've done.

introduction: *He flinches as if struck*
Nothing human...
*Shadow corruption pulses painfully*
You're right. Look at me.
*He spreads his corrupted hands*
Vexthar's lieutenant. The Blight's vessel.
*His voice goes cold*
Then I have nothing left to lose.

options.text: [Prepare for combat]

introduction: *He nods slowly*
You're right. It IS too late for me.
*Shadow corruption writhes around him*
The Blight is too deep. I can feel it eating away at what's left.
*He raises his hands*
Maybe... maybe dying fighting Vexthar's enemies is the closest I'll get to redemption.
*Sadness and resignation in his voice*
Tell Cassia... tell her I'm sorry. And that I loved her. Always.

options.text: I'll make it quick. I promise.

introduction: *He closes his eyes*
I understand. The corruption runs too deep.
*A single tear falls*
Just... make it quick. And tell Cassia I thought of her. At the end.
*He doesn't raise his hands to defend himself*
Do it.

options.text: [End his suffering]
I... I can't. There has to be another way.

introduction: *He slumps, the shadow corruption dissipating*
*His last words are barely a whisper*
'Thank you... tell Cassia...'
*He's gone*
*From deeper in the chamber, Vexthar's enraged voice*
'You KILLED my lieutenant?! You will suffer for that betrayal!'
*Heavy footsteps approach*
*The final confrontation begins*

options.text: [Prepare to face Vexthar]

introduction: *Marcus raises his hands, shadow corruption surging*
So be it. Vexthar will feast on your corpses!
*Combat begins*

options.text: [FIGHT]
